research.interface: Atlas Administration API v2 Project IP Access List resource (media type 2023-01-01)

official_sources.title: Add Entries to Project IP Access List | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/accessList; groupId pattern ^([a-f0-9]{24})$; request body is an array of entries each holding exactly one of ipAddress/cidrBlock/awsSecurityGroup plus optional comment and deleteAfterDate; success is the paginated current list; pagination query params pageNum/itemsPerPage/includeCount

official_sources.title: Add Entries to Project IP Access List | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation (current operation id)
official_sources.used_for: the concurrency rule quoted verbatim: 'This endpoint doesn't support concurrent POST requests. You must submit multiple POST requests synchronously.'

official_sources.title: Return Status of One Project IP Access List Entry | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/accessList/{entryValue}/status; the entryValue path pattern showing CIDR slashes as %2[fF] (percent-encoding required); media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json

official_sources.title: MongoDB Atlas Administration API 2.0 — official OpenAPI specification (latest resource versions)
official_sources.used_for: NetworkPermissionEntry schema (ipAddress/cidrBlock/awsSecurityGroup mutually exclusive, comment maxLength 80, readOnly groupId/links); NetworkPermissionEntryStatus {STATUS: PENDING|FAILED|ACTIVE} with per-state semantics; PaginatedNetworkAccessView {links, results, totalCount}; declared error responses for the POST are 400/401/403/404/429/500 — no 409 Conflict; all error responses are application/json ApiError
official_sources.catalog_correction: the internal catalog assumed 409-on-duplicate for this POST; current official docs document idempotent duplicate absorption and no 409 for this operation

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Rate Limits - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: 429 with errorCode RATE_LIMITED_TOKEN_BUCKET and the {detail, error, errorCode, parameters, reason} body; Retry-After header (seconds) on 429 only; RateLimit-Limit/RateLimit-Remaining may be absent; recommended wait-at-least-Retry-After-then-back-off strategy

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Reference - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: link relations self/next/previous with next omitted on the last page; empty result sets return 200 with results: []; error envelope field meanings

research.language_rationale: The network-automation service this reconciler lives in is TypeScript on Node. MongoDB publishes no official Node Administration SDK — the documented surface for JavaScript shops is the versioned REST API — so the built-in fetch with an explicit dated media type is the credible zero-dependency integration path.

verified_facts: The project IP access list lives at /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/accessList with dated media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json; errors are application/json ApiError.

POST takes a JSON array; each element sets exactly one of ipAddress, cidrBlock, awsSecurityGroup (comment <= 80 chars, optional deleteAfterDate) and the success response is the paginated current list.

The POST endpoint does not support concurrent POSTs — batches must be submitted synchronously; its declared error set is 400/401/403/404/429/500 with no 409, and duplicate values are absorbed idempotently (catalog correction).
Entry status is a separate GET /accessList/{entryValue}/status returning {STATUS} with enum PENDING, FAILED, ACTIVE; PENDING means propagation in progress, FAILED is terminal.
In the status path the CIDR slash must be percent-encoded as %2F; bare IPv4 addresses and sg- ids embed verbatim (per the documented entryValue pattern).
List responses page via links rel=self/next/previous with next omitted on the last page; empty lists are 200 with results: [].
429 responses carry errorCode RATE_LIMITED_TOKEN_BUCKET and a Retry-After header in seconds; clients should wait at least that long before retrying.
pageNum defaults to 1, itemsPerPage defaults to 100 (max 500), includeCount defaults to true.
